mission.title: Install and Configure Ollama
mission.description: Ollama is an AI platform designed for serving large language models (LLMs) such as Kimi-K2.5 and GLM-5. It simplifies the deployment and management of AI models on Kubernetes, making it suitable for developers looking to integrate AI capabilities into their applications. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and resource management.

steps.title: Add Ollama Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Ollama Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Ollama using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add ollama https://ollama.com/helm

helm repo update

```

> This command sets up the Ollama repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Ollama CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Ollama to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://ollama.com/crds/ollama-crds.yaml

```

> This command applies the necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Ollama using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the Ollama platform on your Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and namespace.

```bash

helm install ollama ollama/ollama --namespace ollama --create-namespace --version 0.6.0

```

> Replace 0.6.0 with the desired version if needed.

steps.title: Create an Example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example custom resource to deploy a model using Ollama.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ollama.com/v1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: gemma3
spec:
  model: gemma3
EOF

```

> This command creates a model resource for Gemma 3.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ollama.com/v1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: gemma3
spec:
  model: gemma3
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is Ready
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the deployed model to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get models -n ollama

```

> Look for the status of the model to confirm it is ready.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Ollama
uninstall.description:
This step removes Ollama from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

helm uninstall ollama --namespace ollama

kubectl delete namespace ollama

```

> This command uninstalls the Ollama release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Ollama
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade Ollama to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get models -n ollama -o json > backup-models.json

helm upgrade ollama ollama/ollama --namespace ollama --version 0.6.0

kubectl get models -n ollama

```

> Backup current model configurations.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the models are still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get models -n ollama -o json > backup-models.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'model not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified model does not exist in the Ollama deployment.

**Fix:**
Ensure the model name is correct and that it has been created successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Ollama API'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Ollama service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Ollama pods using 'kubectl get pods -n ollama'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources to deploy the model.

**Fix:**
Scale up your Kubernetes nodes or adjust resource requests for the model.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions for Ollama have not been installed.

**Fix:**
Re-run the command to apply the CRDs: 'kubectl apply -f https://ollama.com/crds/ollama-crds.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved API endpoints.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old model API endpoints.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update any scripts or applications using the old API endpoints to the new ones.

resolution.summary: You should see the model listed with a status of 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get models -n ollama'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ollama.com/v1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: gemma3
spec:
  model: gemma3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster set up with sufficient resources.
